title: verbascum thapsus subsp. montanum
intro: Verbascum thapsus subsp. montanum, also known as the alpine mullein or mountain mullein, is a subspecies of the Verbascum thapsus plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. This biennial plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Verbascum thapsus subsp. montanum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its use in treating skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
